primary.title: Primary Sit — Core Converge
primary.reasoning: Win strength: Narrow. Breaks if: bedding pressure settles deeper toward the backup option. State Game Land 204 stays legal here; 586 m on a mid-slope bench. Primary read: Bench Bedding Edge. Support: terrain continuity. This sit is level with the surrounding pull with a flatter shelf on the side-hill, which creates a more believable hunting setup than a flat random point. It also holds better interior security than a boundary-hugging sit. Species tuning: Whitetail Deer modifier +26.9. Feature-aware signals: bench, edge, cover (+9.5). Dominant-trait read: edge density, cover density, food source, crop edge shaped this species call (+5.4). Whitetail will favor low-pressure edges, bedding cover, mast or crop food, wind-safe access, and terrain funnels that let deer move without exposing themselves. Feature read: active=bench, edge, cover; inactive=ridge, open. Whitetail weighting favors bench-edge-cover security and penalizes exposed open ground. Vegetation read: shrub cover adds workable concealment and modestly strengthens travel and bedding confidence. This read came from terrain fallback rather than direct land-cover data, so confidence stays slightly tempered. Cover proxy: thicker-feeling travel structure adds security to movement here. Feeding proxy: bench-edge and lower-shelf terrain adds a mild destination pull. Behavior Profiles v2: edge-security optimizer adjusted this final selection (+9.7; flow=0.35, security=0.93, visibility=0.09, food=0.71, broken=0.29, low=0.52, flow_offset=0.5). Competitive read: this is a narrow win over the nearest backup because it carries slightly cleaner total terrain fit. Conditional read: this stays viable unless bedding pressure settles deeper toward the backup option.
primary.feature_readout: Feature read: active=bench, edge, cover; inactive=ridge, open. Whitetail weighting favors bench-edge-cover security and penalizes exposed open ground.

corridors.name: Dominant Corridor
corridors.reasoning: Main travel line between the strongest legal sit positions along upper structure.

corridors.name: Secondary Corridor A
corridors.reasoning: Fallback connection between alternate sits when the primary lane is compromised.

notes: Selected bbox is enforced as a hard analysis fence before ranking and viewer payload generation.
Only legal areas can surface primary or alternate sits when verified legal candidates exist.
Restricted and unknown areas remain visible as suppressed near-misses.
Ranking now adds relative terrain trust logic so convergence, shoulder, funnel, bench, and edge reads come from the DEM instead of presentation-only phrasing.
Ranking blends terrain structure, legal gating, timing fit, and wind guard logic.
When no verified legal candidates exist inside the selected bbox, the system falls back to terrain-only review mode instead of crashing.
Provider health now reduces confidence and score when terrain or legal data is degraded.
Vegetation classification now meaningfully changes concealment, bedding confidence, and weak-cover penalties inside sit scoring.

summary.best_time_window: 2.5 hours before sunset through last light

species_profile.label: Whitetail Deer
species_profile.positive_hook: Whitetail will favor low-pressure edges, bedding cover, mast or crop food, wind-safe access, and terrain funnels that let deer move without exposing themselves.
species_profile.caution_hook: For whitetail, downgrade easy-looking spots when wind, human pressure, access exposure, or legal confidence is weak.

invalidators: Wind drifts into the preferred approach lane.
Access pressure rises on the legal edge.
The route forces you through the hot corridor instead of offsetting from it.
Terrain quality or legal verification is too weak to trust the sit call.
